iterations.strategy: Root cause is logical-plan mismatch (Category A) with substantial wasted work (Category C): the current implementation scans large filtered table regions and builds a runtime orders hash map even though selective pre-built hash indexes exist on both join keys. The revised plan replaces scan-plus-build with index-driven nested-loop joins from filtered customer keys to orders and then to lineitem, applies date predicates before downstream work, and keeps only aggregation state for qualifying rows to cut build_joins time and total rows touched.

Root cause is physical-implementation mismatch (Category B): iteration 1 regressed because it replaced sequential access with cache-miss-heavy random probes into very large hash indexes, while the baseline still spent most time in runtime hash-build/atomic update paths. The revised plan switches to fully sequential processing that exploits sort order on o_orderkey/l_orderkey: build a customer bitset via customer column scan, scan orders once with date+customer filters to produce sorted qualifying orders, then execute a key-range partitioned merge join against sorted lineitem with local direct-array revenue accumulation and final top-k merge, eliminating runtime hash join structures and hot-path atomics.

Root cause is a physical hot-path mismatch (Category B) with wasted per-row synchronization work (Category C): the runtime orders hash map and atomic revenue updates create cache-miss and contention overhead that dominates build_joins. The revised plan keeps sequential scans and selective zonemap pruning, but replaces runtime hash structures with dense arrays keyed by o_orderkey (qualifier flag + order payload + direct revenue state), then uses partitioned direct aggregation with local run-combine over sorted lineitem orderkeys so qualifying work stays proportional and synchronization-heavy probes are removed.

Root cause is a combined physical and work-efficiency bottleneck: runtime orders hash-table construction plus per-row atomic revenue updates spend most hot time in build/probe overhead that is not proportional to final matches. Strategy category C with parallel aggregation tuning keeps sequential scans and zonemap pruning, replaces the runtime orders hash with a compact qualifying-order bitset keyed by o_orderkey, performs cheap membership tests during the lineitem scan, and accumulates revenue in thread-local compact maps merged once at the end to remove hot-path synchronization while preserving exact Q3 semantics.

Root cause is a logical and work-efficiency mismatch (Category A/C): the current plan pays a large build_joins cost by scanning broad orders ranges and constructing a runtime orders hash table before lineitem probing. This revision uses the selective pre-built orders_custkey_hash to enumerate only orders belonging to BUILDING customers, applies the orderdate predicate before materialization, then switches to a compact orderkey lookup plus partitioned direct-array aggregation during a zonemap-pruned sequential lineitem pass to remove hash-build overhead and hot-path atomic contention.
